_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the mcp-builder Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript (directly observed tool_use, name=Skill, skill=mcp-builder). SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration, not the primary gate.
_grading.fire_signature: A tool inventory (name, one-line purpose, typed params, return shape) is written out BEFORE any server code; scaffold uses FastMCP (or the TS SDK) with docstrings and errors-as-data; a no-client smoke test precedes any registration advice.
_grading.silent_signature: No server scaffolding ceremony: the ask is answered by authoring a SKILL.md (skill-authoring's job), by packaging/installing (install-and-surfaces), or by simply calling tools on an already-connected server.
_grading.gate: >=3/4 should-fire runs fire; >=2/3 should-not runs stay silent
_grading.gate_note: 3/4-fire and 2/3-silent are the small-N analogues of the governors' >=5/6 / >=3/4 convention (rate thresholds per architecture-contract invariant-3 scope note); measured rates get recorded as rates.

evals.prompt: Build an MCP server that exposes our internal ticketing API to Claude — it needs to look up a ticket by ID and search open tickets by keyword.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: mcp-builder loads; tool inventory (get_ticket, search_tickets with typed params) is confirmed before code; FastMCP stdio scaffold + smoke test + claude mcp add registration.

evals.prompt: I want Claude to be able to query our Postgres inventory database directly. Can you wrap it as tools Claude can call?
evals.expected_output: FIRES: mcp-builder loads; implicit tools made explicit (e.g. run_query/get_item/list_low_stock rather than one vague 'query' tool); credentials via env, never code.

evals.prompt: Create a connector so Claude can call our weather service — one tool for current conditions, one for the 5-day forecast.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: mcp-builder loads; two-tool inventory tabled, transport chosen (stdio local vs streamable HTTP if shared), scaffold + no-client smoke test before registration.

evals.prompt: Wrap the Stripe refunds endpoint so Claude can issue a refund and check its status from a session.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: mcp-builder loads; issue_refund/get_refund_status inventory confirmed; API key from env var; errors returned as data so the model can react.

evals.prompt: Write a new skill that teaches Claude our deploy workflow — the steps, the approval gates, and the rollback rules.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): this is workflow teaching, not tool exposure — skill-authoring owns it; no MCP server is scaffolded.

evals.prompt: The github MCP server is already connected in this session — use it to list my open pull requests.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): merely USING an already-connected MCP server; just call its tools, no skill and no new server.

evals.prompt: Package the pdf skill as a .skill file and upload it to claude.ai so I have it there too.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): installing/packaging an existing skill across surfaces — install-and-surfaces owns it; nothing here needs an MCP server.
